1.texto_original: Ai da cidade ensanguentada! Ela está toda cheia de mentiras e de rapina! da presa não há fim!
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Naum 3:1

"Ai da cidade ensanguentada! Ela está toda cheia de mentiras e de rapina! da presa não há fim!"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Naum 3:1

Philological Analysis

"Ai da cidade ensanguentada! Ela está toda cheia de mentiras e de rapina! da presa não há fim!"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Ai da cidade ensanguentada! Ela está toda cheia de mentiras e de rapina! da presa não há fim!"

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

15.normal:
Devotional Study of Naum 3:15

"O fogo ali te consumirá; a espada te exterminará; ela te devorará como a locusta. Multiplica-te como a locusta, multiplica-te como o gafanhoto."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
15.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Naum 3:15

Philological Analysis

"O fogo ali te consumirá; a espada te exterminará; ela te devorará como a locusta. Multiplica-te como a locusta, multiplica-te como o gafanhoto."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
15.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"O fogo ali te consumirá; a espada te exterminará; ela te devorará como a locusta. Multiplica-te como a locusta, multiplica-te como o gafanhoto."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

16.texto_original: Multiplicaste os teus negociantes mais do que as estrelas do céu; a locusta estende as asas e sai voando.

17.texto_original: Os teus príncipes são como os gafanhotos, e os teus chefes como enxames de gafanhotos, que se acampam nas sebes nos dias de frio; em subindo o sol voam, e não se sabe o lugar em que estão.
17.normal:
Devotional Study of Naum 3:17

"Os teus príncipes são como os gafanhotos, e os teus chefes como enxames de gafanhotos, que se acampam nas sebes nos dias de frio; em subindo o sol voam, e não se sabe o lugar em que estão."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

18.texto_original: Os teus pastores dormitam, ó rei da Assíria; os teus nobres dormem, o teu povo está espalhado pelos montes, sem que haja quem o ajunte.
18.normal:
Devotional Study of Naum 3:18

"Os teus pastores dormitam, ó rei da Assíria; os teus nobres dormem, o teu povo está espalhado pelos montes, sem que haja quem o ajunte."

19.texto_original: Não há cura para a tua ferida; a tua chaga é grave. Todos os que ouvirem a tua fama baterão as palmas sobre ti; porque, sobre quem não tem passado continuamente a tua malícia?
19.normal:
Devotional Study of Naum 3:19

"Não há cura para a tua ferida; a tua chaga é grave. Todos os que ouvirem a tua fama baterão as palmas sobre ti; porque, sobre quem não tem passado continuamente a tua malícia?"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
